subject: Chemistry
title: Alfred Nobel Invents Dynamite
description: A fast breakdown of Alfred Nobel Invents Dynamite for builders.

hook.text: Nitroglycerin was powerful but suicidal to handle. Alfred Nobel found a way to tame it.

buildup.text: Nitroglycerin explodes from the slightest shock. Nobel's own brother Emil was killed in a factory blast.

discovery.text: Nobel mixed nitroglycerin with diatomaceous earth—a chalky powder. The result was stable and safe to handle.

twist.text: He patented dynamite in 1867. It made him incredibly rich—and horrified at how it was used in war.

climax.text: His guilt led him to create the Nobel Prizes—rewarding peace, science, and literature with his fortune.

punchline.text: He tamed an explosion, then funded peace. Nobel's paradox.

quiz.question: How did Nobel make nitroglycerin safe to handle?
options: He mixed it with diatomaceous earth to create dynamite
He froze it into solid blocks
